design_summary: LLVM IR text generation from HIR, with zero native dependencies. Instead of using inkwell (which requires LLVM libraries installed), we generate LLVM IR as textual .ll format directly. The output can be compiled with `llc` and `clang` to produce executables. This approach gets us to working binaries faster, keeps the build dependency-free, and produces standard LLVM IR that can be verified with `llvm-as`. Migration to inkwell for optimization passes is deferred to Phase 2.

design_decision.choice: Option A — Direct LLVM IR text generation
rationale: Zero native dependencies: no LLVM/MLIR libraries needed to build axiom-codegen
Simpler implementation: string-based IR generation is straightforward and debuggable
Full LLVM compatibility: textual IR is the canonical LLVM interchange format
Easy testing: output is plain text, trivially verified with string comparison or regex
Incremental path: can switch to inkwell in Phase 2 without changing the public API
Faster CI: no LLVM build step needed in CI pipeline
tradeoffs: No LLVM validation at generation time (must use llvm-as externally to verify)
No access to LLVM optimization passes (deferred to Phase 2)
Must manually track SSA register numbering
Must manually emit correct LLVM IR syntax (no type-safe builder API)

architecture_overview.approach: alloca-based local variable strategy (not SSA phi nodes) for Phase 1 simplicity. LLVM's mem2reg pass will promote allocas to SSA registers when optimizing. This is the standard approach used by Clang and other frontends.
architecture_overview.register_naming: SSA registers use numbered temporaries (%0, %1, %2, ...) managed by a counter in CodegenContext. Named allocas use the variable name prefix (e.g., %a, %b, %temp).

files_to_create.purpose: Crate root — re-exports public API from submodules

detailed_spec.overview: Re-exports the public codegen function and error types. Declares submodules.
dependencies: axiom-hir (for HirModule and all HIR types)

files_to_create.purpose: Codegen error types with diagnostic integration

detailed_spec.overview: Defines CodegenError using thiserror. Each variant represents a category of code generation failure. Errors carry enough context for useful diagnostics.

types.name: CodegenError
types.doc: Errors that can occur during LLVM IR code generation

variants.name: UnsupportedType
variants.doc: A type was encountered that the codegen does not yet support (e.g., tensors, u128, bf16)

variants.name: UnsupportedExpression
variants.doc: An expression kind was encountered that codegen does not yet handle

variants.name: UnsupportedStatement
variants.doc: A statement kind was encountered that codegen does not yet handle

variants.name: UndefinedVariable
variants.doc: A variable was referenced that has no alloca in the current scope

variants.name: UndefinedFunction
variants.doc: A function was called that has not been defined or declared

variants.name: TypeMismatch
variants.doc: Types did not match where they were expected to (e.g., return type vs actual)

variants.name: InvalidMain
variants.doc: The main function has an invalid signature

variants.name: InternalError
variants.doc: An internal invariant was violated — this is a compiler bug

files_to_create.purpose: LLVM IR text generator — the core of M1.4

detailed_spec.overview: Generates valid LLVM IR text from an HirModule. Uses alloca-based strategy for local variables. Manages SSA register numbering with a monotonic counter. Handles built-in functions (print, print_i64, widen, range) specially.

fields.next_reg: u32 — next SSA register number for unnamed temporaries
fields.next_label: u32 — next label number for basic blocks
fields.variables: HashMap<String, VarInfo> — maps variable names to their alloca info
fields.functions: HashMap<String, FuncInfo> — maps function names to their signatures
fields.string_literals: Vec<String> — collected string constants for global declarations

fields.alloca_name: String — the LLVM name of the alloca (e.g., %a)

internal_types.name: LlvmValue
internal_types.doc: Result of emitting an expression — an SSA register name or immediate

fields.reg: String — the register name (e.g., %3) or literal (e.g., 42)
fields.ty: String — the LLVM type (e.g., i32, i64, float, double)

public_functions.doc: Generate LLVM IR text from an HIR module. Returns the complete .ll file content on success.
algorithm: 1. Create CodegenContext
2. First pass: register all function signatures in ctx.functions
3. Register built-in function signatures (printf, puts)
4. Emit module header: target triple, target datalayout
5. For each function in module.functions: call emit_function()
6. Emit string literal global constants
7. Emit external function declarations (printf, puts)
8. If errors is empty, return Ok(output), else Err(errors)

internal_functions.name: emit_function
algorithm: 1. Reset next_reg to 0, clear variables scope
2. Emit `define {ret_type} @{name}({params}) {`
3. Emit entry label
4. For each parameter: emit alloca, store param into alloca, register in variables
5. Emit block statements via emit_block()
6. Emit `}`

internal_functions.name: emit_block
algorithm: For each statement in block.stmts: call emit_stmt()

internal_functions.name: emit_stmt
algorithm: Match on stmt.kind:
- Let: emit alloca for variable type, emit expression, store result, register in variables
- Assign: look up target variable alloca, emit value expression, store
- Return: emit value expression, emit `ret {type} {value}`
- If: emit condition, emit `br i1 {cond}, label %then_N, label %else_N` (or %merge_N if no else), emit then block, emit br to merge, emit else block if present, emit merge label
- For: recognize range(start, end) pattern, emit alloca for loop var, store start, branch to cond block, emit comparison, branch to body or merge, emit body, increment, branch back to cond
- Expr: emit expression (for side effects like function calls)
- While: emit cond label, emit condition, br to body or merge, emit body, br to cond

internal_functions.name: emit_expr
algorithm: Match on expr.kind:
- IntLiteral: return LlvmValue with literal string and i64 type (default) or contextual type
- FloatLiteral: return LlvmValue with literal string and double type
- BoolLiteral: return LlvmValue with 0 or 1 and i1 type
- StringLiteral: add to string_literals vec, return pointer to global constant
- Ident: look up in variables, emit load from alloca, return loaded register
- BinaryOp: emit lhs, emit rhs, emit appropriate instruction (see binary_op_table), return result register
- UnaryOp: Neg → sub 0, operand; Not → xor operand, 1 (for i1)
- Call: dispatch to built-in handlers or emit generic call instruction

internal_functions.name: emit_builtin_call
internal_functions.signature: fn emit_builtin_call(&mut self, name: &str, args: &[HirExpr]) -> LlvmValue
internal_functions.doc: Handle built-in function calls that need special codegen

builtins.print(msg: str): Emit call to puts() with the string constant pointer
builtins.print_i64(n: i64): Emit call to printf() with format string "%lld\n" and the i64 value
builtins.range(start, end): NOT emitted as a call — recognized and lowered in emit_stmt For handler

internal_functions.name: fresh_reg
internal_functions.doc: Return next numbered register like %0, %1, %2 and increment counter

internal_functions.name: fresh_label
internal_functions.doc: Return next numbered label like then.0, else.0, merge.0 and increment counter

internal_functions.name: hir_type_to_llvm
internal_functions.doc: Convert HIR type to LLVM IR type string

binary_op_table.description: Mapping from (BinOp, type_category) to LLVM instruction. Integer vs float is determined by the LlvmValue.ty of operands.
binary_op_table.notes: Phase 1 treats all integer types as signed (uses sdiv, srem, icmp slt, etc.). Unsigned distinction will be tracked in Phase 2 when we add signedness to the type mapping.

for_loop_lowering.description: The `for i: i32 in range(start, end)` pattern is recognized and lowered to a counted loop.
for_loop_lowering.recognition: The iterable expression must be a Call to an Ident named 'range' with 1 or 2 arguments. range(end) means range(0, end). range(start, end) means [start, end).
llvm_pattern: ; for i: i32 in range(start, end)
%i.next = add i32 %i.val, 1        ; or reload and add

if_else_lowering.description: If/else is lowered using conditional branches and labels. Uses alloca-based approach, no phi nodes needed.
if_else_lowering.return_in_branches: If both branches have a return statement, the merge block may be unreachable. We still emit it with `unreachable` to maintain valid LLVM IR. Alternatively, we track whether a block has been terminated and skip the trailing br.

return_handling.description: Return statements emit `ret {type} {value}`. After a ret, no further instructions should be emitted in the same basic block. The codegen must track whether the current block has been terminated.
return_handling.early_return: When `return` appears inside an if/else block, the ret terminates that block. The branch to merge is suppressed. The merge block is still emitted but may contain only `unreachable` or may be dead.

changes.description: Add axiom-hir as a path dependency
Add axiom-codegen as a path dependency (if not already present)

files_to_modify.purpose: Wire up --emit=llvm-ir and full compilation pipeline

changes.description: Add the llvm-ir emit path after the hir path
changes.location: In the match arm for Some("llvm-ir") and None (full compile)
algorithm: 1. Parse source → AST
2. Lower AST → HIR
3. Call axiom_codegen::codegen(&hir_module)
4. For --emit=llvm-ir: print the LLVM IR string to stdout
5. For full compile (no --emit): write .ll to temp file, invoke clang/llc to compile to binary

expected_llvm_ir: @.str.0 = private unnamed_addr constant [18 x i8] c"Hello from AXIOM!\00"
@.fmt.i64 = private unnamed_addr constant [6 x i8] c"%lld\0A\00"
; for i: i32 in range(2, n + 1)
notes: Register numbers reset per function (each function has its own counter starting from %0)
Named allocas (%a, %b, %temp, %i, %result) use the variable name for readability
Named allocas do NOT consume a numbered register — the name IS the register
The for loop end bound (n+1) is computed once BEFORE the loop and stored in a register
widen(n) is lowered to sext (sign-extend) i32 to i64
print_i64(n) is lowered to printf("%lld\n", n)
Comments are included for readability but are optional
Annotations (@pure, @complexity, @intent, @module) are ignored during codegen in Phase 1
The `if n <= 1 { return widen(n); }` has no else, so we branch to then.0 or merge.0 directly
Since then.0 ends with ret, no `br label %merge.0` is needed after it

module_header.target_triple_detection: Use cfg!(target_os) and cfg!(target_arch) at compile time, or default to x86_64-pc-linux-gnu. On Windows, use x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.
module_header.note: target datalayout is optional for Phase 1 — LLVM can infer it from target triple

external_declarations.note: Only emit declarations for external functions actually used

string_constants.template: @.str.{N} = private unnamed_addr constant [{len} x i8] c"{escaped}\00"
string_constants.example: @.str.0 = private unnamed_addr constant [18 x i8] c"Hello from AXIOM!\00"

format_strings.i64_format: @.fmt.i64 = private unnamed_addr constant [6 x i8] c"%lld\0A\00"
format_strings.i32_format: @.fmt.i32 = private unnamed_addr constant [4 x i8] c"%d\0A\00"
format_strings.f64_format: @.fmt.f64 = private unnamed_addr constant [4 x i8] c"%f\0A\00"
format_strings.note: Only emit format strings that are actually used

alloca_instruction.note: All allocas should be in the entry block for mem2reg compatibility

usage.widen(n: i32) → i64: sext i32 %n to i64 (sign-extend for signed types)

scope_and_variable_management.strategy: Flat HashMap with variable shadowing by overwrite for Phase 1. Nested scopes (e.g., for-loop body introducing `temp`) simply add to the same map. Since AXIOM has explicit types and no closures in Phase 1, this is sufficient.
scope_and_variable_management.named_vs_numbered_registers: Variable allocas use the variable name as the register name (%a, %b, %temp). Intermediate computation results use numbered registers (%0, %1, %2). Named registers and numbered registers occupy separate namespaces in LLVM IR.
scope_and_variable_management.param_handling: Function parameters arrive as SSA values (%name). We immediately alloca+store them so they can be treated uniformly as variables. The alloca name uses the param name with a .addr suffix (e.g., %n.addr) to avoid collision with the parameter name.
scope_and_variable_management.loop_body_allocas: In Phase 1, allocas inside loop bodies are emitted where they appear. This is technically suboptimal (alloca in a loop = stack growth), but LLVM's mem2reg and SROA passes handle this correctly. Phase 2 can hoist allocas to entry block.

print.lowering: Calls C puts() with a pointer to a null-terminated string constant
print.notes: The string is stored as a global constant. puts() adds a newline automatically.

print_i64.lowering: Calls C printf() with format string "%lld\n"
print_i64.notes: Format string uses %lld for 64-bit signed integer. \0A is newline.

widen.notes: Phase 1 only supports i32→i64. Phase 2 will generalize to arbitrary widening.

range.axiom_signature: fn range(start: i32, end: i32) -> iterator
range.lowering: NOT emitted as a function call. Recognized syntactically in the for-loop lowering. The start and end expressions are extracted and used as loop bounds.
range.notes: range(end) is sugar for range(0, end). Both forms must be recognized.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.issue: SSA register numbering with named allocas
edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: In LLVM IR, named registers (%name) and unnamed registers (%0, %1, ...) are in SEPARATE namespaces. Named allocas do NOT consume a number from the unnamed counter. However, within a function, unnamed registers must be numbered sequentially starting from 0 (or from the next number after parameters).
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Track named vs numbered separately. The next_reg counter only advances for unnamed temporaries.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: In LLVM IR, function parameters are implicitly numbered starting from %0. If a function has 2 params, the first unnamed register must be %2.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: When using named parameters (%n, %a, %b), this is not an issue — named params don't consume numbered registers. We use named parameters exclusively for function params.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: If a return statement is inside an if-then block, subsequent code in the function is still reachable via the else/merge path. But if both if and else branches return, the merge block is unreachable.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Track whether the current block has been terminated (ret or br). If terminated, skip emitting further instructions. Emit `unreachable` in dead merge blocks if needed.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: Phase 1 emits `alloca` inside loop bodies (e.g., `let temp: i64 = b;` inside for loop). Each loop iteration allocates new stack space. This is technically a stack leak.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Acceptable for Phase 1. LLVM's optimization passes handle this. Phase 2 should hoist all allocas to the function entry block.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: HIR IntLiteral has i128 value but no type annotation. When used in `let x: i32 = 42;`, the literal must be emitted as i32. When used in `let y: i64 = 0;`, as i64.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Use the type from the context (let binding type, function return type, other operand type in binary op). Default to i64 when no context is available.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: LLVM IR requires float literals in specific format: decimal with at least one digit on each side of the decimal point, or hexadecimal. For example, 0.0 is valid, .0 is not.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Format float values using Rust's `{:.1}` or similar to ensure valid LLVM float literal syntax. Or use hex format for exact representation.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: The target triple affects calling conventions, name mangling, and external function availability. puts/printf work on both platforms but the triple must match.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Auto-detect from the host platform at codegen time. Use x86_64-pc-windows-msvc on Windows, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu on Linux, x86_64-apple-macosx on macOS.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: LLVM string constants need the exact byte length including the null terminator. Escaped characters count as ONE byte.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Count the actual bytes of the UTF-8 encoded string, then add 1 for the null terminator.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: The loop variable `i` should be scoped to the loop body. If there's a variable `i` in the outer scope, the loop variable should shadow it within the loop, but the outer `i` should be restored after the loop.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Phase 1: simple HashMap overwrite. If shadowing is needed, save and restore the old variable info. Phase 2: proper scoped symbol table.

edge_cases_and_gotchas.description: In `for i in range(2, n+1)`, the end bound `n+1` must be evaluated ONCE before the loop starts, not on every iteration.
edge_cases_and_gotchas.solution: Emit the end bound expression before the loop, store result in a temporary, and use that temporary in the loop condition.

implementation_order.rationale: Core infrastructure before emitting any real code

implementation_order.description: Implement emit_function with parameter handling and emit_block
implementation_order.rationale: Function structure is needed before statements

implementation_order.description: Implement emit_expr for IntLiteral, FloatLiteral, BoolLiteral, Ident, BinaryOp, UnaryOp
implementation_order.rationale: Expressions are needed by all statement types

implementation_order.description: Implement emit_stmt for Let, Assign, Return, Expr
implementation_order.rationale: Basic statements enable the simplest programs

Implement for-loop lowering with range() recognition
Implement built-in functions: print, print_i64, widen
implementation_order.rationale: I/O and type conversion needed for sample programs

implementation_order.description: Implement string constant emission and external declarations
implementation_order.rationale: Required by print and printf calls
CLI integration for testing and AC-1.4.2/AC-1.4.3

acceptance_criteria_mapping.AC-1.4.4: Steps 1-9: generated IR is syntactically valid (verified by llvm-as if available)

phase_2_migration_notes.inkwell_migration: The public API `codegen(module: &HirModule) -> Result<String, Vec<CodegenError>>` is designed so that the implementation can switch from text generation to inkwell without changing callers. With inkwell, we would return the LLVM IR string via `module.print_to_string()`. The internal implementation changes entirely, but the API stays the same.
phase_2_migration_notes.optimization_passes: Once using inkwell, we can run LLVM optimization passes: mem2reg (promote allocas to SSA), simplifycfg (clean up branches), instcombine (algebraic simplifications), gvn, licm, etc.
phase_2_migration_notes.alloca_hoisting: Phase 2 should hoist all allocas to the function entry block for better optimization.
phase_2_migration_notes.signedness_tracking: Phase 2 should track signed vs unsigned integer types to use correct LLVM instructions (sdiv vs udiv, icmp slt vs icmp ult).
